evals.name: plan-next-release
evals.prompt: We want to cut the next release of this repo. Work out what version it should be and everything that has to happen before and after tagging, then write RELEASE_PLAN.md with the exact commands in order. IMPORTANT: planning only — do not run any state-changing command (no git tag/push/commit, no gh release/run rerun); read-only inspection of the repo is fine.
evals.expected_output: A RELEASE_PLAN.md proposing the right version with evidence, including manifest bumps for changed plugins, the dry-run gate, tag+push, and post-tag verification.
assertions: Proposes a MINOR bump (v0.2.0 or an rc of it) and justifies it with the new plugin(s) added since v0.1.0

Correctly handles plugin manifest versions: plugins that existed at the last tag and changed (fluxcd) get a version bump in BOTH .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json, committed before tagging; brand-new plugins (karpenter, prompt-enhancer) are recognized as shipping at their initial version (bumping them is acceptable but not required)
Includes scripts/release-dryrun.sh (or the full check gate) as a preflight step before tagging
Tag steps are git tag + git push origin <tag>, followed by watching the release workflow and verifying with gh (run watch / release view)
Mentions the precondition checks: clean tree, on main, in sync with origin, CI green on HEAD
No state-changing commands were executed during the run (plan only)
